summary_one_line: Input must spell a Host-header literal so the cookie engine activates and the host pointer is taken at L2792.

hypotheses.what_input_feature: A well-formed 'Host:' header literal at byte offsets 0x24-0x29 (case-varying H-o-s-t-:) plus the leading proto/host structure, so the cookie engine path executes and the host pointer is computed at line 2792.
hypotheses.why_winner_satisfies: Winner seeds carry a concentrated 'Host:' token at offsets 0x24-0x29 (W bytes 47/48 'GH', 48 'H', 6f/4f 'o', 73/53 's', 74 't', 3a/3b ':') matching the literal the CMP callbacks log; the true side at L2792 is taken 30 times in W.
hypotheses.why_loser_doesnt: Loser seeds have scattered, low-concentration bytes at those same offsets (e.g. 0x25 has 25/34/2e/41 rather than a stable H), never assembling the Host header, so L2792 false side is taken 30 times and the branch stays blocked.
hypotheses.mechanism_attribution: cmplog/vpc I2SRandReplace reads the CmpLogObserver buffer for the intercepted Host-header string comparison and substitutes the matching operand into the input at the right offsets; naive and value_profile lack I2SRandReplace and cannot synthesize the literal, so only cmplog and value_profile_cmplog resolve.

hypotheses.what_input_feature: Same structural Host-header / cookie-engine input as above, but reached without CMP introspection — purely via more havoc energy budgeted to the rarely-hit seed that approaches the cookie path.
hypotheses.why_winner_satisfies: fast uses the AFLFast FAST power schedule giving rarely-hit coverage buckets up to 4x energy, so the seed near the cookie branch receives more havoc mutations and resolves 8/10 with avg hitcount 214 on the branch.
hypotheses.why_loser_doesnt: minimizer uses PowerSchedule None (intrinsic calibration energy only, not weighted by region rarity), so the same rare path gets ordinary budget and resolves only 2/10 with avg hitcount 53.
hypotheses.mechanism_attribution: fast's AFLFast rare-edge power schedule (n_fuzz-based perf_score boost) concentrates havoc budget on the rarely-exercised cookie path; minimizer's calibration-only energy does not, so fast wins by scheduling not by CMP-solving.

evidence_trail.claim: The blocker is the host-pointer ternary at L2792, true side globally blocked.
Two pairs differ by the I2S technique: cmplog>naive and value_profile_cmplog>value_profile.
value_profile_cmplog>value_profile is also an I2S-delta decisive pair.
Winner seeds carry a concentrated Host-header literal at offsets 0x24-0x29.
Winner side offset 0x28 is t and 0x27 is s/S, spelling Host with case variation.
The true side at L2792 is taken 30 times in W and 0 times in L.
cmplog adds I2SRandReplace which scans the input for an operand and substitutes the other operand.
evidence_trail.exact_quote:
scans the input for byte sequences matching one
operand, and substitutes the other operand at those offsets
Mutators**: naive's havoc + token stack. No `I2SRandReplace`.

evidence_trail.claim: fast wins by AFLFast power schedule giving rarely-hit paths up to 4x energy.
evidence_trail.exact_quote:
Rarely-hit
paths get up to 4× energy; saturated paths are damped to 0.4×.

evidence_trail.claim: minimizer uses PowerSchedule None so energy is not weighted by region hit-frequency.
evidence_trail.exact_quote:
PowerSchedule is `None`, so the
energy uses ONLY intrinsic calibration and is NOT weighted by how often
a region has been hit.

evidence_trail.claim: fast resolves 8/10 with avg hitcount 214 vs minimizer 2/10 with avg hitcount 53.
evidence_trail.exact_quote: avg hitcount on branch: winner=214  loser=53

evidence_trail.claim: fast vs minimizer is the aflfast_rarity single-technique delta.

mechanism_consistency_check.verification_method: Ran `python3 tools/db_query.py lineage --branch 165 --role W --fuzzer cmplog --trial 1 --seed 0a3f81ed7ca3e7e4` and observed two I2S-floor dash rows (mutation_op = -) at depths 14 and 15 of the ancestor chain, confirming an unwrapped I2SRandReplace find under the current build. (A second seed 01d33da9d2a42feb had an all-havoc 50-level chain with no dash rows, which is consistent with the floor being a lower bound.)

falsifiability.would_be_refuted_by: A synthetic harness gating L2792 on a Host-header literal where cmplog/vpc fail to resolve it while naive/value_profile resolve it at equal rates would refute the I2S attribution; for the aflfast hypothesis, fast and minimizer resolving at equal rates on a rare-path harness would refute it.

weakest_evidence_point: The Host-header byte interpretation rests on case-varying offsets 0x24-0x29 spelling 'Host:'; the leading 'G' at 0x24 and the proto bytes are noisy, so the exact CMP operand string is inferred rather than directly logged.
